title: SCP desktop status view model
description: Stable, UI-facing snapshot produced by buildRunViewModel() in src/core/status-view.mjs. The desktop monitor app and the optional status bridge render this directly. It is intentionally display-oriented: raw prompts, stdout/stderr, env, and full command bodies are kept out by default; commands and verification snippets are reduced to display-safe summaries. The shape covers both a single-run view (mode 'run') and a list view (mode 'list'). Nested optional properties are documented but not enforced exhaustively — consumers should tolerate additional fields and null gaps (a missing/unknown value is null, never an exception).

schema.description: Versioned contract identifier for the desktop run view model.

mode.description: 'run' for a single run snapshot; 'list' for the runs overview. null when undetermined.

runId.description: Stable run identifier, or null when not yet assigned.

runDir.description: Filesystem path to the run artifact directory, or null.

status.description: Machine status resolved from runtime + counts.

displayStatus.description: Human-friendly status label suitable for direct rendering in a status bar or tray.

statusTone.description: Suggested UI tone/color for the status. 'active' = in progress, 'positive' = healthy/done, 'warning' = blocked or stale, 'negative' = failed, 'neutral' = no signal.

progressHint.description: One-line progress summary, e.g. '3/5 tasks, 1 failed, 1 active'. null when there is nothing to summarize.

activeTaskCount.description: Number of currently active (in-flight) subagent tasks.

lastUsefulEventAt.description: ISO timestamp of the most recent forward-progress event (heartbeat, checkpoint, command, phase/task transition). null when no events are available.

stalenessMs.description: Milliseconds since lastUsefulEventAt (or, in list mode, since the oldest active-task heartbeat). null when not computable. Compare against staleThresholdMs to flag a stalled live run.

staleThresholdMs.description: The freshness threshold in milliseconds used for stale classification. Compare stalenessMs against this shared threshold; summary-only tasks do not become stale solely because they lack heartbeats.

recovered.description: Present and true when the run was recovered after a crash/restart.

error.description: Short, snippet-sized error summary when the run failed.

tasks.description: Task view models for the run's tasks.

activeTasks.description: In-flight subagent process records, sorted stably to avoid snapshot jitter. Observability='live' means freshness/stale logic may use heartbeats/events; observability='summary-only' means the task may be active but only summary/terminal data is expected, so missing heartbeat data alone must not mark it stale.

recentEvents.description: Bounded tail of recent event view models (newest last).

tokenEvidence.description: Measured (never self-reported) token/cost evidence. measured=false when no usage numbers are available.

artifacts.description: Display-safe artifact paths (run dir, summary, per-task result/event log paths).

outputDir.description: List mode: the runs output directory.

taskView.description: A single task result reduced to a display-safe view. Command bodies are redacted to summaries; no prompt/stdout/stderr/env is surfaced.

commandsRun.description: Display-safe command summaries. `label` is the program name; `command` is a redacted, truncated snippet — never the full command body.

runtime.description: Optional. Which agent runtime executed this task. 'claude' = a Claude subagent driven through SCP heartbeats/events; 'codex' = a Codex worker task. null when undetermined or not applicable. Consumers must tolerate absence/null; absence does not imply 'claude'.

dispatcher.description: Optional. How the task was dispatched. 'scp-claude' = dispatched via the SCP Node MCP runtime as a Claude subagent; 'codex-worker' = dispatched as a Codex worker. null when undetermined. Consumers must tolerate absence/null.

workerType.description: Optional. Resolved worker/agent type for display and filtering (for example 'huoshan-worker', 'zhipu-worker', 'normal-worker', or a Claude task role). Opaque to SCP; null when not provided.

workerAlias.description: Optional. Human-friendly alias or nickname that resolved to workerType (for example 'huoshan'). Opaque to SCP; null when not provided.

fallbackApplied.description: Optional. True when a requested Codex worker alias fell back to another worker type (for example zhipu-worker -> normal-worker). This does not describe UI rendering fallback. null when not applicable.

observability.description: Optional. 'live' = real heartbeats/events may be tracked; 'summary-only' = only summary/terminal visibility is expected. summary-only is not a failure state and must not be treated as stale solely because heartbeat data is absent.

activeTaskView.description: Display-safe view of an in-flight task. These records are stable-ordered for snapshot rendering. runtime/dispatcher/workerType/workerAlias/fallbackApplied/observability use the same semantics as taskView.

runtime.description: Optional. Which agent runtime owns this active task. 'claude' = a Claude subagent driven through SCP heartbeats/events; 'codex' = a Codex worker task. null when undetermined.

dispatcher.description: Optional. How the task was dispatched. 'scp-claude' = dispatched via the SCP Node MCP runtime as a Claude subagent; 'codex-worker' = dispatched as a Codex worker.

workerType.description: Optional. Resolved worker/agent type for display and filtering (for example 'huoshan-worker', 'zhipu-worker', 'normal-worker', or a Claude task role). Opaque to SCP; null when not provided.

workerAlias.description: Optional. Human-friendly alias or nickname that resolved to workerType (for example 'huoshan'). Opaque to SCP; null when not provided.

fallbackApplied.description: Optional. True when a requested Codex worker alias fell back to another worker type. This does not describe UI rendering fallback.

observability.description: Optional. 'live' = freshness can be evaluated from real heartbeats/events. 'summary-only' = only summary/terminal visibility is expected, so missing heartbeat data alone must not mark the task stale.

eventView.description: A single JSONL event reduced to render-relevant fields.

sequence.description: Run-global monotonic cursor for the merged run event stream. Cursor reads using afterSequence only include events with numeric sequence values; null-sequence events remain snapshot-only.
